title: Ukraine Strikes Russian Black Sea Energy Hub at Novorossiysk
detail: Ukraine struck Russia's Black Sea energy hub at Novorossiysk, expanding its sustained campaign against Russian energy and logistics infrastructure beyond oil refineries. The strike targets one of Russia's key Black Sea export terminals, which handles significant volumes of crude oil and petroleum products. The attack is part of Ukraine's broader deep-strike strategy aimed at degrading Russia's ability to generate oil revenues that fund the war effort, following a series of strikes on Baltic port facilities at Ust-Luga and Primorsk in late March 2026.

title: Ukraine Claims Russia Supplied Iran with Targeting Coordinates for Israeli Energy Grid
detail: Ukraine has claimed that Russia provided Iran with precise targeting coordinates for Israeli energy grid infrastructure, significantly escalating concerns over the depth of Russia-Iran military cooperation. The allegation, if confirmed, would represent a direct Russian role in enabling potential strikes on Israeli critical infrastructure — a major escalation beyond previously documented arms transfers and drone technology sharing between Moscow and Tehran. The disclosure adds a new dimension to the war's geopolitical fallout, linking the Ukraine conflict to Middle Eastern security dynamics.
